F1_employee_profiles.current_state: 96 employees tracked across 56 work codes, showing avg times per employee per code. StdDev indicates consistency.
F1_employee_profiles.impact: Variance between fastest/slowest employee can be 2-5x. Using team avg instead of individual profiling could misestimate labor by 30-50%.
F1_employee_profiles.recommendation: Add employee skill profiles to estimator. For repeat customers, suggest assigning proven fast performers. Flag high-variance codes (stddev > 50% of mean) as risky.

F2_employee_timeline.current_state: 118 total employees tracked. 17 active last 30 days, 93 haven't worked in 1+ year. Some employees span 10+ years.
F2_employee_timeline.impact: Estimating with inactive employees = ghost capacity. Overestimating available workforce by ~20-30% if not accounting for turnover.
F2_employee_timeline.recommendation: Mark employees inactive after 90 days no activity. Exclude from crew planning. Add 'returning employee' flag if they come back (may need refresher training).

F3_role_shifts.current_state: 61 employees changed primary work codes over time. 306 total transitions detected across quarters.
F3_role_shifts.impact: Role transitions = learning curves. New role performance typically 20-40% slower for first 5-10 jobs. Estimating at veteran speed = underestimating hours.
F3_role_shifts.recommendation: Add 'experience_level' field: Junior (<10 jobs), Intermediate (10-30), Senior (30+). Apply learning curve multiplier (1.3x junior, 1.1x intermediate) when assigning new roles.

F4_department_capacity.current_state: 148 employee-code pairs active in last 6 months. Shows current weekly capacity per employee per skill.
F4_department_capacity.impact: Not tracking capacity = accepting jobs you can't staff. Avg 30-40 hrs/week/employee across all codes. Overcommitting by 20% = missed deadlines.
F4_department_capacity.recommendation: Use capacity data for scheduling. Flag 'high demand' codes (>80% capacity utilization). Add buffer time (1.2x) for codes near capacity. Consider hiring if sustained >90% utilization.

F5_active_roster.current_state: 25 employees active in last year. Top performers log 2000+ hrs/year. 20 are multi-skilled across 3+ codes.
F5_active_roster.impact: Multi-skilled workers = scheduling flexibility. Single-skill workers = bottleneck risk. Losing a high-volume worker = losing 10-15% of dept capacity.
F5_active_roster.recommendation: Cross-train single-skill employees. Prioritize retention of high-volume multi-skilled workers. Create succession plan for top 5 performers in each dept.

F6_performance_tiers.current_state: Analyzed top 5 work codes. Speed variance between fastest and slowest is typically 2-3x. Fast performers are 50-70% faster than slow performers.
F6_performance_tiers.impact: Assigning slow performer to 100-hour job = 200 actual hours. Cost overrun of $3000-5000 per job (at $30-50/hr). Across 50 jobs/year = $150k-250k lost margin.
F6_performance_tiers.recommendation: Assign fast performers to high-margin or time-critical jobs. Use slow performers for training/mentoring roles or less critical work. Consider performance-based pay to incentivize speed without sacrificing quality.
